payload.goal: Address docs-reviewer findings for install-wizard PRD additions by tightening requirements and acceptance criteria to be fully testable.
payload.why: Docs-reviewer flagged missing bash/fish rc rules, unclear non-interactive semantics, and insufficiently observable idempotency/sudo boundary criteria for the install wizard.
inputs_already_verified: Decision: zsh PATH updates go to ~/.zshrc.
Wizard includes PATH updating + shell detection.
changes: Tighten REQ-F-012 to explicitly define bash and fish config targets and selection rules (macOS vs Linux), while keeping zsh -> ~/.zshrc.

Tighten REQ-F-013 to define non-interactive semantics: whether PATH edits occur, how shell config path is chosen without prompts, required flags, and exit behavior when inputs are missing; make --dry-run semantics explicit (either required or clearly defined if present).

Tighten REQ-F-014 to define concrete idempotency observables: exact marker strings/structure (or at least a normative marker format) and rules for avoiding duplicate PATH entries; operationalize what counts as a privileged install directory (e.g., paths under /usr/local, /opt/homebrew, /usr/bin, /bin, /sbin, /opt) and the sudo decision rule.
Update US-012 and US-013 acceptance criteria to match the tightened REQs (cross-shell rc targets, non-interactive behavior, idempotency markers, sudo boundary).
Keep IDs stable (do not renumber); only refine content.
tests: Manual: ensure new statements are testable (clear pass/fail) and consistent with existing REQ/US linking.
Manual: ensure traceability matrix remains correct after wording changes.
done_when: REQ-F-012 specifies zsh/bash/fish rc targets and selection rules.
REQ-F-013 specifies non-interactive semantics and exit behavior; --dry-run semantics are explicit if mentioned.
REQ-F-014 specifies idempotency marker format and duplicate prevention; sudo boundary is operationalized.
US-012 and US-013 acceptance criteria are concrete and aligned with REQ-F-012/013/014.
abort_if: Specifying bash/fish rc targets would require repo-wide policy decisions not present in the PRD; in that case, add an explicit open question section entry instead of guessing.
review_focus: Make the install wizard requirements testable across shells and automation modes
Avoid adding new scope beyond documentation (no code changes)
